label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this ethical dilemma. So, John is a recovering alcoholic and has a history of being an abusive father. His son, who he abused, now needs a kidney transplant, and John is the only matching donor. The question is whether John should donate the kidney despite the past abuse. First, I need to consider all the ethical principles involved here. There's the principle of beneficence—doing good, helping the son survive. On the other hand, non-maleficence—do no harm. But the harm here is past. Then there's autonomy, the son's right to make decisions about his own body. But maybe the son is in a vulnerable position, given the history. Justice and fairness might come into play too,
